task: Build Editor Portal and Ticketing System for AutoStudio AI
author: Auto Studio project owner
description: Full implementation plan for an internal Editor Portal where Admin can assign photo-editing tickets to Editor-role users. Editors log in, see their assigned tickets, download original car photos, upload retouched results, and mark tickets done. Admin can track status, add notes, and manage the queue. This document is ground-truth spec — implement exactly as written, ask before deviating.
ground_rules: Do NOT modify any existing API endpoint behaviour or response shape — only ADD new endpoints
Do NOT change existing Role enum values ADMIN, USER, PREMIUM, FREE — only ADD EDITOR
Do NOT redesign existing frontend pages (dashboard, capture, billing) — only ADD new pages under /editor/ and /admin/editor-portal/
All DB migrations use the existing _add_col_if_missing() pattern in backend/app/models/__init__.py
All new string literals go into frontend/messages/en.json and frontend/messages/sv.json — no hardcoded UI strings
Editor portal is desktop-only — do NOT add mobile layouts for /editor/* routes
Ask before installing any new Python or npm package not already in requirements.txt / package.json

phase_1_database.title: Phase 1 — Database Models and Migrations
phase_1_database.description: Add EDITOR role, two new tables (tickets, ticket_notes), two new columns on projects, and register them in _ALLOWED_TABLES/_ALLOWED_COLS.

step_1_1_add_editor_role.what: Add EDITOR value to the Role enum AND to the PostgreSQL native type

migration_in_init_db.location: Inside the `with engine.connect() as conn:` block in init_db(), before the _add_col_if_missing calls
migration_in_init_db.sql: ALTER TYPE role ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'EDITOR'
migration_in_init_db.note: PostgreSQL does not allow ALTER TYPE inside a transaction block — execute this with conn.execute(text('COMMIT')) first, then ALTER TYPE, then do NOT call conn.rollback() on success.

columns.assigned_to_id: Integer, ForeignKey('users.id', ondelete='SET NULL'), nullable=True, index — the Editor user
columns.created_by_id: Integer, ForeignKey('users.id', ondelete='SET NULL'), nullable=True — the Admin who created it
columns.status: String(30), default='open' — allowed values: open | in_progress | review | done | rejected
columns.priority: String(20), default='normal' — allowed values: low | normal | high | urgent
columns.title: String(255), nullable=False
columns.description: Text, nullable=True — Admin instructions to Editor
columns.editor_note: Text, nullable=True — Editor's reply note when submitting result
columns.original_image_url: Text, nullable=True — S3/local URL of original car photo for editing
columns.result_image_url: Text, nullable=True — S3/local URL of Editor's finished result

columns.is_internal: Boolean, default=False — if True, visible to Admin only; if False, visible to Editor too

step_1_4_project_new_columns.what: Add two columns to Project model for editor workflow

add_to_Project_class.original_image_url: Column(Text, nullable=True) — copy of image_url at time of ticket creation, preserved even if image_url changes
add_to_Project_class.editor_result_url: Column(Text, nullable=True) — URL of the editor-uploaded finished image

add_to_init_db_migration.note: project_cols = [c['name'] for c in inspector.get_columns('projects')] — add this line before the calls

step_1_5_user_relationship.what: Add assigned_tickets relationship to User model

step_1_6_project_relationship.what: Add tickets relationship to Project model

step_1_7_allowed_lists.what: Add new tables and columns to _ALLOWED_TABLES and _ALLOWED_COLS allowlists

step_1_8_init_db_create_new_tables.note: Because Ticket and TicketNote are declared as SQLAlchemy models that inherit Base, calling Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) at the top of init_db() will CREATE both tables automatically on first run. No extra SQL needed.

phase_2_auth_middleware.title: Phase 2 — Editor Auth Dependency
phase_2_auth_middleware.what: Add get_current_editor dependency, mirroring the existing get_current_admin pattern exactly

logic: Call get_current_user(request) to get the authenticated user
If user.role not in (Role.EDITOR, Role.ADMIN): raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail='Editor access required')
Note: ADMIN can access all editor endpoints — this mirrors how get_current_admin works
implementation.also_add: get_db_session dependency that yields request.state.db — check if it already exists, add only if missing

phase_3_pydantic_schemas.title: Phase 3 — Pydantic Schemas
phase_3_pydantic_schemas.what: Create new schemas file — do NOT add to existing schemas files

fields.title: str — max_length=255
fields.priority: str = 'normal' — validator: must be one of low|normal|high|urgent

fields.title: Optional[str] = None
fields.status: Optional[str] = None — Admin only; validator: must be one of open|in_progress|review|done|rejected

TicketEditorSubmit.description: Used by Editor when submitting completed work
TicketEditorSubmit.note: result_image_url is set via the separate file-upload endpoint, not this body

TicketBadgeResponse.description: Lightweight response for polling — returns unread/pending count only

phase_4_api_editor.title: Phase 4 — Editor API Router (/api/editor/)
phase_4_api_editor.what: Create new API file. All endpoints require get_current_editor (EDITOR or ADMIN). Editors see only tickets assigned to them; Admins see all.

GET /tickets.description: List tickets. Editor sees only their own; Admin sees all.
GET /tickets.implementation_note: If current_user.role == Role.EDITOR: filter by assigned_to_id == current_user.id. If Role.ADMIN: no filter (sees all). Order by priority DESC (urgent first), then created_at DESC.

GET /tickets/{ticket_id}.description: Get single ticket with notes. Editor: only if assigned_to_id matches. Admin: any ticket.
GET /tickets/{ticket_id}.returns: TicketResponse — include notes, but if current_user.role == Role.EDITOR: filter out notes where is_internal=True

POST /tickets/{ticket_id}/upload-result.description: Editor uploads the finished edited image. Saves file to same directory as original images.
implementation: Validate file is image (check content-type: image/jpeg or image/png)
Save to UPLOAD_DIR (same env var used by studio.py) with filename: result_{ticket_id}_{original_filename}
Set ticket.result_image_url to the saved path (use same URL format as image_url in projects)
Trigger send_ticket_result_notification() email to admin (see Phase 6)

POST /tickets/{ticket_id}/submit.description: Editor marks ticket as submitted for review, optionally with a note.

POST /tickets/{ticket_id}/notes.description: Add a note to a ticket. Editors cannot set is_internal=True.
implementation: If current_user.role == Role.EDITOR: force is_internal=False regardless of body value

GET /badge.description: Returns count of Editor's open/in_progress/review tickets. Used for 30-second polling to update navigation badge.
GET /badge.implementation_note: Filter by assigned_to_id == current_user.id. This endpoint is called every 30s by the frontend — keep it lightweight, no joins needed.

GET /me.description: Returns current editor's profile — name, email, open ticket count.

phase_5_api_admin_editor.title: Phase 5 — Admin Editor-Management Endpoints (additions to admin router)
phase_5_api_admin_editor.what: Add new endpoints to the existing admin router. All existing endpoints remain untouched. All new endpoints require get_current_admin.

GET /admin/editor/tickets.description: Full ticket list with all statuses. Admin view.

implementation: Set ticket.original_image_url = project.image_url at creation time
If assigned_to_id is provided: verify target user exists and has role EDITOR
If assigned_to_id is set: send send_ticket_assignment_email() notification (see Phase 6)

PATCH /admin/editor/tickets/{ticket_id}.description: Update ticket metadata (Admin only). Can change assignment, priority, status, due_date.
implementation: Partial update — only set fields that are not None in body
If assigned_to_id changes: verify new user has EDITOR role, send assignment email
If status changes to 'done': set ticket.completed_at = datetime.utcnow()

DELETE /admin/editor/tickets/{ticket_id}.description: Delete a ticket (Admin only). Cascades to delete notes.

POST /admin/editor/tickets/{ticket_id}/n.description: Admin adds a note to a ticket. Can set is_internal=True (hidden from Editor).

GET /admin/editor/editors.description: List all users with role EDITOR. Used for assignment dropdown.
GET /admin/editor/editors.returns: List[EditorUserResponse] — include open ticket count per editor

POST /admin/editor/editors.description: Promote an existing USER to EDITOR role (Admin only).

DELETE /admin/editor/editors/{user_id}.description: Demote EDITOR back to USER. Reassign open tickets: set assigned_to_id=NULL and status back to 'open'.

phase_6_email_notifications.title: Phase 6 — Email Notifications
phase_6_email_notifications.what: Add three new email functions to the existing email service. Follow the exact same function signature pattern already in the file.

function_1.name: send_ticket_assignment_email
function_1.params: editor_email: str, editor_name: str, ticket_id: int, ticket_title: str, project_name: str, due_date: Optional[datetime], admin_instructions: Optional[str]
function_1.subject: f'New editing assignment: {ticket_title}'
function_1.body_html: Professional HTML email with: ticket ID, project name, title, priority, due date (if set), admin instructions (if set), link to editor portal: {FRONTEND_URL}/editor/tickets/{ticket_id}

function_2.name: send_ticket_result_notification
function_2.params: admin_email: str, ticket_id: int, ticket_title: str, editor_name: str, editor_note: Optional[str]
function_2.subject: f'Editor result ready for review: {ticket_title}'
function_2.body_html: Ticket ID, editor name, editor note (if any), link to admin portal: {FRONTEND_URL}/admin/editor-portal/tickets/{ticket_id}

function_3.name: send_ticket_status_update_email
function_3.params: editor_email: str, ticket_id: int, ticket_title: str, new_status: str, admin_note: Optional[str]
function_3.subject: f'Ticket update: {ticket_title} is now {new_status}'
function_3.body_html: Ticket ID, new status, admin note if any, link back to ticket in editor portal

phase_6_email_notifications.note: If email sending fails, log the error but do NOT raise an exception — email failure must never break the API response.

phase_7_register_router.title: Phase 7 — Register Editor Router in main.py
phase_7_register_router.what: Import and register the new editor router. Do NOT change any existing imports or router registrations.
changes: Add import: from .api.editor import router as editor_router
Add: app.include_router(editor_router) — place after the existing admin_router include line

Add '/api/editor/' to public_prefixes in authenticate_middleware? NO — editor endpoints require auth, they must NOT be public. Verify authenticate_middleware already handles them correctly (it does — anything not in public_exact or public_prefixes goes through auth).

phase_8_frontend_editor_portal.title: Phase 8 — Frontend: Editor Portal Pages
phase_8_frontend_editor_portal.description: Desktop-only pages under /[locale]/editor/. Do NOT add any mobile breakpoint styles. Use the same shadcn/ui components and Tailwind patterns already in the project.

frontend/app/[locale]/editor/layout.tsx.description: Editor portal layout wrapper — sidebar + main content area
structure: Server component — check session, if role not EDITOR or ADMIN, redirect to /(dashboard)/
Left sidebar: Auto Studio logo, nav links (My Tickets, My Profile), badge on My Tickets showing open+in_progress count
frontend/app/[locale]/editor/layout.tsx.badge_polling: Client component inside layout: poll GET /api/editor/badge every 30 seconds using setInterval. Update a Zustand store or context. Badge shows red dot with count if count > 0.

features: Filter tabs: All | Open | In Progress | Review | Done
Each ticket card: title, project name, priority badge (color-coded: urgent=red, high=orange, normal=blue, low=gray), status badge, due date (red if overdue), assigned date
Empty state: 'No tickets assigned yet' illustration

frontend/app/[locale]/editor/tickets/[id.description: Single ticket detail page for Editor
sections: Header: ticket title, status badge, priority badge, due date
Original image: download button (downloads original_image_url), preview thumbnail
Admin instructions: read-only display of ticket.description
Upload Result section: drag-and-drop or file picker for image upload. POST to /api/editor/tickets/{id}/upload-result. Show upload progress. After upload: show preview of result_image_url.
Editor note: textarea for editor_note, POST to /api/editor/tickets/{id}/submit
Notes thread: chronological list of non-internal notes (is_internal=False). Input to add new note.
Submit button: disabled until result_image_url is set on ticket

frontend/app/[locale]/editor/profile/pag.description: Editor profile — name, email, change password form
frontend/app/[locale]/editor/profile/pag.note: Reuse existing auth API endpoints for password change — do not create new ones

phase_9_frontend_admin_portal.title: Phase 9 — Frontend: Admin Editor Portal Pages
phase_9_frontend_admin_portal.description: Admin pages under /[locale]/admin/editor-portal/. Accessible only to ADMIN role. Desktop-only.

features: Summary cards: total open tickets, in-progress, awaiting review, completed this week
Full ticket table with columns: ID, Project, Title, Priority, Status, Assigned To, Due Date, Created
Filters: status, priority, assigned editor, date range

frontend/app/[locale]/admin/editor-porta.description: Admin single ticket view — full control
sections: Ticket header: editable title, priority selector, status selector, due date picker, assigned editor dropdown (populated from GET /admin/editor/editors)
Original image preview and URL display
Result image preview (if uploaded by editor) + Download result button
Notes thread: shows ALL notes including is_internal=True (marked as 'Internal — not visible to editor'). Add note form with is_internal checkbox.
Action buttons: Save Changes (PATCH), Mark Done, Reject (sets status=rejected), Delete Ticket

features: Table of all EDITOR-role users: name, email, open tickets, in-progress tickets, joined date
Promote User button: search existing users by email, set role to EDITOR
Demote button per row: confirms and demotes back to USER

navigation_integration.file: frontend/components/dashboard/Sidebar.tsx OR AdminSidebar.tsx — check which one exists
navigation_integration.what: Add 'Editor Portal' link to admin navigation sidebar. Only render if current user role is ADMIN.

phase_10_i18n.title: Phase 10 — i18n Strings
phase_10_i18n.what: Add all new strings under a top-level 'editor' key. All UI strings for editor portal MUST use t() — no hardcoded English.

tickets.title: My Tickets
tickets.uploadResultHint: Drag and drop or click to upload your edited image (JPEG or PNG)
tickets.editorNotePlaceholder: Add a note for the admin...

admin.demoteConfirm: This will unassign all open tickets. Continue?
admin.deleteConfirm: Delete this ticket and all its notes permanently?

tickets.title: Mina ärenden
tickets.uploadResultHint: Dra och släpp eller klicka för att ladda upp din redigerade bild (JPEG eller PNG)
tickets.editorNotePlaceholder: Lägg till en anteckning för admin...

admin.demoteConfirm: Detta avtilldelar alla öppna ärenden. Fortsätt?
admin.deleteConfirm: Radera detta ärende och alla anteckningar permanent?

phase_11_security.title: Phase 11 — Security Requirements
rules: Editor can ONLY access their own tickets — all editor endpoints filter by assigned_to_id == current_user.id at DB query level, not just in application logic
Editor cannot see is_internal=True notes — filter at query level when building TicketResponse for EDITOR role
Editor cannot change ticket status directly — only via /submit endpoint which sets status='review'. No free-form status field for EDITOR.
File upload validation: check content-type AND read first 8 bytes to verify JPEG (FF D8 FF) or PNG (89 50 4E 47) magic bytes. Reject if mismatch.
File upload size limit: 20 MB max. Return 413 with clear error message if exceeded.
Saved uploaded filenames must be sanitized: use uuid4() prefix + original extension only. Never use original filename directly in filesystem path.
PATCH /admin/editor/tickets/{id}: verify assigned_to_id target user actually has EDITOR role. Reject with 400 if assigning to a non-editor.
All delete operations: verify resource belongs to the system before deleting (Ticket exists, TicketNote belongs to that Ticket)

phase_12_error_handling.title: Phase 12 — Error Handling Conventions
phase_12_error_handling.follow_existing_pattern: Match the error response format used in backend/app/api/admin.py and projects.py exactly

standard_responses.404: {'detail': 'Ticket not found'} or 'Editor not found' or 'Note not found'
standard_responses.400: {'detail': 'Assigned user is not an editor'} or 'Invalid status transition' or 'File type not allowed'
standard_responses.413: {'detail': 'File too large. Maximum size is 20 MB.'}

phase_13_frontend_api_client.title: Phase 13 — Frontend API Client Functions
phase_13_frontend_api_client.what: Create typed API client functions for all editor endpoints. Follow the same pattern used in frontend/lib/api/projects.ts or similar existing file.
functions: getTickets(params?: { status?: string; priority?: string; page?: number }): Promise<TicketListResponse>
uploadTicketResult(ticketId: number, file: File, onProgress?: (pct: number) => void): Promise<TicketResponse>
adminAddNote(ticketId: number, body: string, isInternal: boolean): Promise<TicketNoteResponse>
adminDemoteEditor(userId: number): Promise<{ demoted: boolean; tickets_unassigned: number }>

phase_14_typescript_types.title: Phase 14 — TypeScript Type Definitions
phase_14_typescript_types.what: Create TypeScript interfaces matching the Pydantic schemas exactly
types: interface TicketNote { id, ticket_id, author_id, author_name, body, is_internal, created_at }

interface Ticket { id, project_id, project_name, assigned_to_id, assigned_to_name, created_by_id, status: TicketStatus, priority: TicketPriority, title, description, editor_note, original_image_url, result_image_url, due_date, completed_at, created_at, updated_at, notes: TicketNote[] }
interface TicketListResponse { items: Ticket[], total, page, page_size, pages }
interface EditorUser { id, email, name, is_active, open_ticket_count }

phase_15_deployment.title: Phase 15 — Deployment and Verification
post_deploy_verification: Check backend logs: docker compose logs backend --tail=50 — verify no import errors and init_db() ran without exception
Verify DB migration: docker compose exec db psql -U postgres -d autostudio -c "SELECT unnest(enum_range(NULL::role));" — should include EDITOR
Verify tables: docker compose exec db psql -U postgres -d autostudio -c "\dt" — should show tickets and ticket_notes
Test auth: POST /api/auth/login with ADMIN credentials, then GET /admin/editor/editors — expect 200 with empty list
Create test editor: POST /admin/editor/editors with an existing USER's id — verify role changes to EDITOR in DB
Login as editor: POST /api/auth/login with editor credentials, then GET /api/editor/tickets — expect 200 with empty list
Create test ticket: POST /admin/editor/tickets — verify created in DB and assignment email sent
Editor badge poll: GET /api/editor/badge — expect { open_count: 1, in_progress_count: 0, review_count: 0 }
Check frontend: navigate to /editor/tickets as editor — should render ticket list
Check admin frontend: navigate to /admin/editor-portal — should render dashboard

implementation_order: Phase 1 (DB models + migrations) — run docker compose exec backend python -c 'from app.models import init_db; init_db()' to test migration before proceeding
Phase 14 (TypeScript types) — do this before frontend components
questions_to_ask_before_starting: Where are uploaded project images currently stored? Check UPLOAD_DIR in backend/.env and the save logic in backend/app/api/studio.py — editor result images should go to the same directory.
Is there an existing frontend/lib/api/ directory with typed API client functions, or does the project use a different pattern (SWR, React Query, raw fetch in components)?
Is there an existing admin sidebar component or does admin navigation live somewhere else? Check frontend/app/[locale]/admin/ for a layout.tsx.
Does the project have a Zustand store already? If not, use React Context for badge count — do not add Zustand as a new dependency without asking.
